page: Amazon Web Services m8g.xlarge is a general-purpose virtual machine powered by the custom AWS Graviton processor family. Built on the arm64 architecture, this instance features 4 dedicated physical cores utilizing the AWS Neoverse-V2 model running at 2.8 GHz. It is equipped with 16.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced allocation of 4.0 GB per core. The processor includes a cache hierarchy consisting of 64 KB L1 data cache, 2 MB L2 cache, and 36 MB L3 cache. The instance operates on the AWS Nitro hypervisor, lacks local storage, and provides a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks indicate average capabilities across standard workloads. The single-core and multi-core stress-ng tests yield scores of 1707.29 and 6818.16 respectively, placing them in the middle tier of tested hardware. Memory bandwidth is measured at 462.02 GB/sec for cached small-block reads and 96.39 GB/sec for uncached large-block reads. Single-threaded compression tasks show strong performance, with gzip compression reaching 30.34 GB/sec and bzip3 decompression reaching 14.17 GB/sec. However, the instance exhibits weaker performance in cryptographic operations, with a Passmark encryption score of 753.29, and in LLM inference tasks, where text generation for a 7B model is limited to 6.51 tokens per second.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the arm64 architecture and dedicated core allocation offer a resource-dense profile for general-purpose computing without the overhead of hyperthreading. The balanced memory-to-vCPU ratio makes this instance suitable for application servers, static web serving, and database operations, as demonstrated by a static web serving throughput of 10.08 GB/sec and 9.39 million Redis SET operations per second. It is less suitable for intensive cryptographic workloads or large-scale machine learning inference.
description: Amazon Web Services m8g.xlarge is a general-purpose virtual machine powered by an arm64-based AWS Graviton processor. It features 4 dedicated physical cores using the AWS Neoverse-V2 model running at 2.8 GHz, paired with 16.0 GB of system memory. Operating on the AWS Nitro hypervisor, the instance provides a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps and does not include local storage. Benchmark results show average performance in general compute and memory tasks, with strong single-threaded compression speeds, though cryptographic and LLM inference capabilities are relatively weak. With its balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, the m8g.xlarge is well-suited for general-purpose workloads such as static web serving, application hosting, and database operations like Redis, where it achieves 9.39 million SET operations per second.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m8g.xlarge is a general-purpose arm64 server featuring 4 dedicated Graviton Neoverse-V2 cores, 16 GB of RAM, and a 2 Gbps network baseline, optimized for balanced workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m8g.xlarge is a general-purpose arm64 instance with 4 dedicated Graviton cores, 16 GB RAM, and a 2 Gbps network baseline.
tagline: An arm64 general-purpose instance featuring dedicated Graviton cores and balanced memory allocation for standard application hosting and web serving.
bullet_points: Powered by AWS Graviton Neoverse-V2 arm64 processor with 4 dedicated physical cores
Equipped with 16.0 GB of system memory and 36 MB L3 cache
Delivers 2 Gbps baseline network bandwidth on the AWS Nitro hypervisor
Strong single-threaded compression performance with 30.34 GB/sec in gzip benchmarks
Suited for static web serving, application hosting, and Redis database operations
